_comment: operon-plugin hooks. Phase 6 fix: PreToolUse now uses `type: command` (not `type: mcp_tool`). The mcp_tool wiring caused a chicken-and-egg under real Claude Code: every tool call -- INCLUDING calls to operon's own MCP tools -- fires PreToolUse, which dispatched via MCP, which itself required MCP-tool routing, surfacing as 'MCP server operon not connected' errors during the connection race. Hookify's reference plugin (Anthropic's example) uses type=command for the same reason. The `pretooluse-wrapper` script resolves a python interpreter and invokes `pretooluse.py`, which reuses the same `operon_mcp_server.rules` module and `guardrail_log.jsonl` audit writer that the hidden `evaluate` MCP tool uses. The hidden `evaluate` MCP tool stays in the server's registry for in-process testing and non-hook use cases. Matcher: limited to Bash|Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit -- the side-effectful tool surface. Read-only tools and operon's own MCP tools are NOT gated (the hook would recurse on the latter). Per the channels-reference docs, `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` interpolates to the plugin install root.
